questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the perimeter of the rectangle shown with length $8$ and width $3$.

figure.caption: Rectangle
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='40' width='120' height='50' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><text x='95' y='105' font-size='8'>l=8</text><text x='12' y='68' font-size='8'>w=3</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The perimeter of a square of side $9$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Perimeter of a square $=4\times\text{side}$.

**Working.** $4\times 9 = 36$.

**Answer.** $36$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The least common multiple (LCM) of $6$ and $8$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** LCM is the product of all prime factors at highest powers.

**Working.** $6=2\cdot 3$, $8=2^3$; LCM $=2^3\cdot 3=24$.

**Answer.** $24$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of $10, 20, 30, 40$ and $50$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average = sum divided by count.

**Working.**
$$\frac{10+20+30+40+50}{5}=\frac{150}{5}=30$$

**Answer.** $30$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Convert $72$ km/h into m/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiply km/h by $\tfrac{5}{18}$ to get m/s.

**Working.** $72\times\dfrac{5}{18}=20$ m/s.

**Answer.** $20$ m/s.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The line graph shows a value rising from $20$ to $50$. Compute the percentage increase (%).

figure.caption: Line graph
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><polyline points='40,90 100,75 160,40' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='40' cy='90' r='3' fill='#dc2626'/><circle cx='160' cy='40' r='3' fill='#dc2626'/><text x='45' y='138' font-size='8'>20 -> 50</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\%\text{ increase}=\dfrac{\Delta}{\text{initial}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{50-20}{20}\times100=\frac{30}{20}\times100=150\%$$

**Answer.** $150\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many rectangles (of all sizes) are there in a strip of three unit cells in a row (shown)?

figure.caption: A horizontal strip divided into three equal unit cells.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='15' y='20' width='150' height='40' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='65' y1='20' x2='65' y2='60' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='115' y1='20' x2='115' y2='60' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count rectangles spanning $1$, $2$ or $3$ cells.

**Working.** Single cells $=3$, pairs $=2$, the full strip $=1$; total $=3+2+1=6$.

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A cube painted on all faces is cut into $27$ identical small cubes ($3\times3\times3$). The number of small cubes with exactly two painted faces is _____ .

figure.caption: A 3 by 3 by 3 cube shown with grid lines on its visible faces.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two-face cubes lie along the edges (not corners) of a $3\times3\times3$ cube.

**Working.** A cube has $12$ edges, each contributing one such cube: $12\times1=12$.

**Answer.** $12$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An arrow rotates $90^\circ$ clockwise each step: up, right, down, ... The next arrow points

figure.caption: Three arrows pointing up, right and down, followed by a question mark.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Continue the $90^\circ$ clockwise rotation.

**Working.** After down, a further clockwise quarter-turn points to the left.

**Answer.** Left (west).

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The arrow shown points to the right (east). After rotating it $90^\circ$ anticlockwise, it points

figure.caption: A horizontal arrow pointing to the right.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='40' x2='120' y2='40' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='120,40 105,32 105,48' fill='#ef4444'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $90^\circ$ anticlockwise turn sends east to north.

**Working.** Right rotated anticlockwise becomes upward.

**Answer.** Upward (north).

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Eₙ = E₀(0.1)ⁿ, with E₀ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$12000\times0.1^{2}=120.$$

**Answer.** $120$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: In a reactor, $30\,\text{mg/L}$ of biomass is produced while $120\,\text{mg/L}$ of substrate is consumed. The observed yield $Y_{X/S}$ is _____ mg/mg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Y_{X/S}=\dfrac{\Delta X}{\Delta S}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{30}{120}=0.25.$$

**Answer.** $0.25$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: Which conditions worsen ground-level air pollution by limiting dispersion?

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="10" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">altitude</text><text x="245" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">temperature →</text><line x1="120" y1="160" x2="220" y2="30" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3" stroke-dasharray="5 3"/><text x="222" y="34" font-size="8" fill="#475569">DALR (dry adiabatic)</text><line x1="150" y1="160" x2="200" y2="30" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.6"/><text x="172" y="50" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">ELR (inversion)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Environmental lapse rate vs. dry adiabatic lapse rate — inversion atmosphere.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Inversions, low mixing height and calm winds all trap pollutants; a strong superadiabatic lapse improves dispersion.

**Working.**
First three.

**Answer.** Inversion, low mixing height, calm winds

**Answer.** $6\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: Drinking-water standards require E. coli to be ABSENT in any 100 mL sample. A membrane-filtration test of a 100 mL sample of treated water yields 8 E. coli colonies. The correct interpretation is that the water
options: fails the potability standard and is unsafe to drink
is potable, since 8 is a low count
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The standard is zero E. coli per 100 mL; 8 colonies/100 mL exceeds it, signalling recent faecal contamination — the water is unsafe.

**Working.**
Compare the measured count to the zero-tolerance standard.

**Answer.** Fails / unsafe

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: Select all statutes/instruments that are central environmental legislation in India.
options: The Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974
The Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The Water Act (1974), Air Act (1981) and EP Act (1986) are the core environmental statutes; the Companies Act is corporate law.

**Working.**
First three are environmental law.

**Answer.** Water, Air and EP Acts

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: Bacteria grow exponentially with specific growth rate $\mu=0.231\,\text{h}^{-1}$. The doubling (generation) time is _____ hours.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 180" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="18" x2="38" y2="158" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="158" x2="312" y2="158" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="28" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">ln X</text><text x="270" y="174" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><path d="M38 150 L80 148 L150 60 L230 40 L300 95" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.7"/><text x="50" y="142" font-size="7" fill="#1e293b">lag</text><text x="105" y="100" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">log (μ_max)</text><text x="215" y="34" font-size="7" fill="#1e293b">stationary</text><text x="278" y="110" font-size="7" fill="#dc2626">death</text></svg>
figure.caption: Batch microbial growth curve.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $t_d=\dfrac{\ln 2}{\mu}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.231}=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{h}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Decomposable waste of $1000\,\text{kg}$ yields landfill gas at $0.15\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg}$. The total gas generated is _____ m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><rect x="30" y="40" width="280" height="86" fill="#a16207" opacity="0.5"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="10" fill="#65a30d"/><rect x="30" y="126" width="280" height="14" fill="#1e3a8a" opacity="0.6"/><rect x="30" y="140" width="280" height="10" fill="#0f172a"/><text x="170" y="26" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">final cover / vegetation</text><text x="170" y="86" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">compacted MSW + daily cover</text><text x="170" y="137" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">leachate collection</text><text x="170" y="148" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">composite liner (clay+HDPE)</text><path d="M315 60 q14 0 14 14 q0 10 -8 18" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="318" y="56" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">gas vent</text></svg>
figure.caption: Engineered sanitary landfill: cover, waste cell, liner and leachate system.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gas $=$ mass $\times$ specific yield.

**Working.**
$$1000\times0.15=150.$$

**Answer.** $150\,\text{m}^3$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $3\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 265</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$3\times265=795.$$

**Answer.** $795$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $10\,\text{t}$ of methane (GWP$_{100}=28$). Its carbon-dioxide-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 280</text><rect x="190" y="145.71428571428572" width="46" height="4.285714285714286" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="141.71428571428572" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 10</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄ → CO₂-e (t)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Methane converted to CO₂-equivalent via GWP.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e $=$ mass $\times$ GWP.

**Working.**
$$10\times28=280.$$

**Answer.** $280\,\text{t CO}_2\text{-e}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: Two sources $68\,\text{dB}$ and $73\,\text{dB}$ at a point. Combined level is _____ dB.

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: In a nitrifying reactor the ammonia-nitrogen falls from $40\,\text{mg/L}$ to $6\,\text{mg/L}$ as it is oxidised to nitrate. Taking the stoichiometric requirement as $4.57\,\text{g O}_2$ per g of N nitrified, the oxygen demand exerted by nitrification is _____ mg/L.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Nitrification (NH₄⁺ → NO₃⁻) consumes 4.57 g O₂ per g N. Demand $=\Delta N\times4.57$.

**Working.**
$$(40-6)\times4.57=155.38\,\text{mg/L}.$$

**Answer.** $155.38\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=150\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.5</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 40</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.5\times150}{40+150}=0.395.$$

**Answer.** $0.395$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: An aeration tank: flow $Q=10000\,\text{m}^3/\text{d}$, influent BOD $S_0=250\,\text{mg/L}$, volume $V=2000\,\text{m}^3$, MLSS $X=3000\,\text{mg/L}$. The F/M ratio is _____ d⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 350 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="60" y="40" width="110" height="70" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="115" y="78" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Aeration</text><text x="115" y="92" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">MLSS, V</text><circle cx="240" cy="75" r="34" fill="rgba(22,163,74,0.10)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="240" y="72" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Clarifier</text><path d="M20 70 l40 0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="22" y="62" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">Q, S0</text><path d="M170 70 l36 0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M274 70 l44 0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="290" y="62" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">Qe, Se</text><path d="M240 109 l0 36 -180 0 0 -35" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="120" y="158" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">return sludge Qr</text><path d="M250 140 l40 0" stroke="#7c2d12" stroke-width="1.2"/><text x="255" y="135" font-size="8" fill="#7c2d12">waste Qw</text></svg>
figure.caption: Activated-sludge: aeration basin, secondary clarifier, sludge recycle.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\dfrac{F}{M}=\dfrac{QS_0}{VX}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{10000\times250}{2000\times3000}=0.417.$$

**Answer.** $0.417\,\text{d}^{-1}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays first-order with $k=0.3\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Reading the initial concentration $C_0$ from the figure, its concentration after $7$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 170" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="120" y="100" width="60" height="50" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.25" stroke="#1e293b"/><rect x="120" y="30" width="60" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t = 0</text><text x="150" y="96" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">C₀ = 100</text><text x="150" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial concentration C₀ marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$100e^{-0.3\times7}=12.25.$$

**Answer.** $12.25$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Read the Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations from the bar chart. The total hardness as CaCO₃ (eq. wt Ca 20, Mg 12.15) is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">60</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Ca²⁺</text><rect x="180" y="114" width="50" height="36" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="110" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">18</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Mg²⁺</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to CaCO₃ (×50/eq.wt) and sum, with both concentrations read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$60\times\dfrac{50}{20}+18\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=224.1.$$

**Answer.** $224.1$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A facility emits CO₂, CH₄ (GWP 28) and N₂O (GWP 265); the three masses (t) are shown on the bar chart. The total carbon footprint is _____ t CO₂-e.

**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$50+2\times28+0.5\times265=238.5.$$

**Answer.** $238.5$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A field device estimates wastewater BOD within minutes by measuring the respiration of immobilised microbes through a dissolved-oxygen electrode. The biological recognition element and the transducer are, respectively,
options: immobilised microorganisms and the dissolved-oxygen electrode
the DO electrode and the microbes
questions.solution:
**Concept.** In a microbial BOD biosensor the immobilised microbes are the bio-recognition element; the O₂ they consume is sensed by the DO electrode (the transducer).

**Working.**
Recognition = microbes; transducer = DO electrode.

**Answer.** Microbes; DO electrode

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Ammonia-bearing water is chlorinated. From the breakpoint-chlorination curve shown, read the breakpoint dose. To leave a free residual of $1.5\,\text{mg/L}$, the chlorine that must be applied is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="4" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="232" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose (mg/L) →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L205 160 L300 80" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="205" y1="55" x2="205" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="205" cy="160" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="170" y="50" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="186" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">8</text><text x="250" y="92" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>
figure.caption: Breakpoint dose marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond the breakpoint, extra chlorine appears as free residual: applied = breakpoint dose + desired free residual.

**Working.**
$$8+1.5=9.5.$$

**Answer.** $9.5\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: Which statement about the EIA process in India is correct?
options: Public consultation is a mandatory stage for Category A projects
EIA is optional for all projects
Scoping precedes the identification of the project

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 90" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="8" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="38" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Screening</text><path d="M68 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="78" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="108" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Scoping</text><path d="M138 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="148" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="178" y="44" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Impact</text><text x="178" y="55" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">prediction</text><path d="M208 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="218" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="248" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">EMP</text><path d="M278 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="288" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="318" y="44" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Review &</text><text x="318" y="55" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">clearance</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sequential stages of the EIA process.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Public consultation (public hearing + written responses) is a statutory stage for most Category A and B1 projects under EIA Notification 2006.

**Working.**
Public hearing is mandatory.

**Answer.** Public consultation is mandatory

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays first-order with $k=0.2\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Reading the initial concentration $C_0$ from the figure, its concentration after $4$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 170" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="120" y="110" width="60" height="40" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.25" stroke="#1e293b"/><rect x="120" y="30" width="60" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t = 0</text><text x="150" y="96" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">C₀ = 80</text><text x="150" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial concentration C₀ marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$80e^{-0.2\times4}=35.95.$$

**Answer.** $35.95$
